mapping.a_element: two-stage-action MDP over token placement and remasking (a Markov decision process)
mapping.b_element: finite reversible Markov chain underlying Wilson's algorithm with exponential killing

mapping.a_element: sequential order in which masked positions are unmasked during generation
mapping.b_element: Markov trajectory of the evolving set up to its exit from the punctured domain

brief_justification: The only overlap is that both are set on a Markov process: A optimizes a decomposed policy-gradient (token term + masking term) over a two-stage-action MDP to train a diffusion language model, whereas B derives determinantal/Schur-complement root identities and heat-kernel localization bounds for random spanning forests via Wilson's loop-erased walk with killing. There is no reward/policy-gradient optimization in B and no spanning-forest/determinantal machinery in A, so the transferable machinery differs despite the shared 'Markov' vocabulary.
